verbGlossary.ACCEPTS: {source} or client formally accepts completed {target} resolution or closeout deliverable
verbGlossary.ASSIGNS: {source} or CM delegates {target} to the responsible party for resolution
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED AGAINST: {source}measure compared against an industry or portfolio safety metric
verbGlossary.CITED FOR: {source} formally identified as the responsible party on a safety or {target}
verbGlossary.COMPLETES: {source} finishes {target} resolution and returns it for re-inspection
verbGlossary.CONDUCTS: {source} physically performs an {target}, test, or commissioning activity on site
verbGlossary.CORRECTS: {source} remediates {target}, punch item, or non-conforming work
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {source}measure statistically relates to a quality or safety metric
verbGlossary.DEVELOPS: {source} creates an action plan or corrective strategy in response to findings
verbGlossary.EVALUATES: {source} or authority reviews individual {target} checklist items for pass/fail status
verbGlossary.EXECUTES: {source} carries out the assigned {target} or action plan task
verbGlossary.IDENTIFIES: {source} discovers and documents a coordination issue, {target}, or design conflict
verbGlossary.INITIATES: {source} creates a new observation, finding, or {target} in the system
verbGlossary.INSPECTS: {source} performs a formal review of work in place against code, spec, or safety standards
verbGlossary.INVESTIGATES: Regulatory authority or safety team conducts a formal inquiry into an incident
verbGlossary.INVOLVED IN: {source} has personnel or work connected to a reported {target}
verbGlossary.ISSUES: {source} or responsible party formally issues {target}, deficiency notice, or NCR
verbGlossary.MANAGES: {source} oversees the full lifecycle of a quality or {target} from open to close
verbGlossary.MANDATES: {source}requires {target} or scheduled inspection by code or law
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source}performance metric derived from quality or {target} data
verbGlossary.MONITORS: {source} performs ongoing surveillance of environmental or commissioning conditions
verbGlossary.NOTIFIED OF: {source} formally informed of an incident, violation, or significant {target}
verbGlossary.PERFORMS: Testing lab or monitoring firm executes the physical test and produces the report
verbGlossary.PROVIDES: {source} or fabricator furnishes test reports, mill certs, or witness statements as documentation
verbGlossary.RAISES: {source} flags a coordination or constructability issue for resolution
verbGlossary.RECEIVES: {source} receives a formal {target} notice requiring response or corrective action
verbGlossary.REPORTS: {source} creates and submits an {target} report to the appropriate authorities or owner
verbGlossary.RESPONSIBLE FOR: {source} identified as the accountable party for an {target}, deficiency, or NCR
verbGlossary.REVIEWS: {source} evaluates {target}, test report, or non-conformance for acceptance or disposition
verbGlossary.SCHEDULES: {source} or CM sets the date and scope for a planned {target} or third-party visit
verbGlossary.SUBMITS TO: {source} presents work or materials for third-party or special {target}
verbGlossary.TESTS: Testing lab performs physical tests on materials or assemblies against specification requirements
verbGlossary.WITNESSES: {source} is present during a test or {target} to verify procedures and results
